page: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-4800s-amd is an AMD EPYC-based virtual server belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 24 shared vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled as a 4800 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics placing in the top 25%. PassMark CPU testing corroborates this with a score of 51801.15, also in the top 25%. Memory performance is notable for uncached large-block operations, where read bandwidth reaches 488.87 GB/sec and write bandwidth reaches 225.61 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 10%. Conversely, cached small-block read bandwidth and PassMark database operations perform in the average tier.

The shared CPU allocation presents a potential performance variability tradeoff compared to dedicated CPU instances, though the high memory density and large NVMe storage capacity offer strong resource density for memory-intensive workloads. This configuration is appropriate for large-scale databases, memory-cached web serving, and data processing tasks that require significant local storage and memory capacity without requiring dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-4800s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered virtual server within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 24 shared vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and a 4800 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance delivers strong CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core benchmarks ranking in the top 25%. Uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths are highly competitive, placing in the top 10% at 488.87 GB/sec and 225.61 GB/sec respectively. However, the shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability. With an 8.0 GB per core memory ratio and substantial local NVMe storage, this server is designed for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and large-scale data processing workloads that do not require dedicated GPU hardware.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-4800s-amd is an AMD EPYC virtual server featuring 24 shared vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and a 4800 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong top-25% CPU performance and top-10% uncached memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-4800s-amd is an AMD EPYC server with 24 shared vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 4800 GB NVMe storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: An AMD EPYC virtual server combining high-capacity NVMe storage with dense memory for data-intensive and memory-heavy workloads.

192.0 GB RAM providing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio
